title: Lastrea obovata
taxonomy: Lastrea obovata is classified as a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This genus, Lastrea, belongs to the larger group known as the Thelypteridoideae subfamily. Lastrea obovata is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and has been introduced to other parts of the world. The species is further classified as belonging to the Thelypteridaceae family's genus Lastrea, and more specifically, the Lastrea obovata species. This classification can be seen in various taxonomic databases and sources, although classifications may vary slightly.
uses: Lastrea obovata is known to have several medicinal uses. It is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. In some cultures, the plant is also used to treat respiratory problems and skin conditions. The plant's extracts have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its medicinal effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential benefits and risks of using Lastrea obovata for medicinal purposes.
